title: Dean's Forum: Context and Imagination: Reading the Bible Today
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
October 20, 2019

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This sermon falls on the Twenty-First Sunday after Pentecost in Ordinary Time (the long green season between Pentecost and Advent). The RCL readings for this date would have included Exodus 33:12-23, Psalm 99, 1 Thessalonians 1:1-10, and Matthew 22:15-22 (the "render unto Caesar" passage about taxes and allegiance). The focus on biblical interpretation in the sermon title aligns well with Ordinary Time's emphasis on sustained Christian teaching and formation.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the post-General Convention 2018 period, having made significant decisions on marriage equality and transgender inclusion. Bishop Robert J. O'Neill was serving the Diocese of Colorado (position held 2015-2022). No major diocesan controversies were prominent in mid-October 2019, though the broader Anglican Communion remained fractured over LGBTQ+ inclusion and biblical interpretation—exactly the interpretive questions Greer's sermon title suggests addressing.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing mild fall weather in mid-October with no major weather events that week. Colorado political focus was on the 2020 election cycle, with Senator Cory Gardner facing significant pressure from Democratic challengers. Downtown Denver and the Capitol Hill neighborhood were relatively stable, though homelessness and affordable housing remained ongoing civic concerns that often intersected with religious community work.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The Trump impeachment inquiry dominated headlines, with the House Intelligence Committee conducting depositions the week of October 14-18, particularly focused on the Ukraine scandal. The stock market was volatile amid trade war concerns with China. The 2020 Democratic primary race was intensifying, with Elizabeth Warren rising in polls and candidates debating healthcare, climate change, and student debt. The World Series was underway (Houston Astros vs. Washington Nationals, with the Nationals winning on October 30).

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The Turkish military operation in northeastern Syria ("Operation Peace Spring") had begun on October 9, 2019, causing significant humanitarian displacement and international concern, with the U.S. withdrawing troops from the region. The Hong Kong protests were ongoing and escalating in intensity with clashes between demonstrators and police. Brexit negotiations continued with Boris Johnson's government pursuing various withdrawal proposals ahead of the October 31 deadline.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

The zeitgeist was one of deep polarization and interpretive disagreement across American institutions—political, religious, and social. The sermon's explicit focus on "imagination" and "reading" Scripture reflected broader anxieties about how communities establish shared meaning in a fragmented media landscape. The mid-fall moment carried both the weight of mounting election-year tension and the traditional rhythm of churches preparing for Advent.
